channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer told world leaders on Sunday that they need to step up to a “once in a generation moment” for the security of Europe, at a summit in London to discuss ending Russia's war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A topsy-turvy Oscar season concludes Sunday. Here's what to look for at Sunday's show
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — After a topsy-turvy Oscar season in which frontrunners were constantly shuffled, old tweets hobbled a top contender and space was held for “Wicked,” the 97th Academy Awards get underway Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Oscar fever for Brazil's Fernanda Torres has made her this year's Carnival muse
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — HOLD FOR 10 AM EST, SUNDAY MARCH 2 Brazil's Carnival muse this year isn't one of the divas or drum queens parading with the Rio de Janeiro samba schools. It's Fernanda Torres, who's competing for the best actress Oscar on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Republicans once maligned Medicaid. Now some see a program too big to touch
item.description: There's a mid-March deadline to avoid a partial government shutdown, and hesitation is surfacing among congressional Republicans about potential cuts to Medicaid.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP pushes ahead with citizenship voting bill. Some state election officials say it's problematic
item.description: The centerpiece election legislation from congressional Republicans would require voters to prove their citizenship when registering.
item.dc:creator: Christina A. Cassidy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's next first speech to Congress is bound to have little resemblance to his last first one
item.description: When President Donald Trump speaks to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday, his words are bound to be far different from when he addressed lawmakers at the beginning of his first term.
item.dc:creator: Calvin Woodward, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The world's most famous sled dog race is longer than ever. Here's a look at Iditarod, by the numbers
item.description: Thanks to a lack of snow, this year's edition of the Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race needed a new route across the Alaska wilderness. The course change adds more than 100 miles (161 kilometers), so teams of dogs will have to pull their mushers over 1,100 miles (1,770 kilometers) to the finish line.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: FedEx plane makes emergency landing after bird strike
item.description: “FedEx Flight 3609 from Newark to Indianapolis experienced a bird strike during takeoff," a FedEx spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal weather and oceans agency touches people's daily lives in unnoticed ways, so massive firings there will likely cause needless deaths and a big hit to America's economy, according to the people who ran it.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FedEx plane catches fire midair before making emergency landing
item.description: A Fedex cargo caught fire midair and then made a dramatic emergency landing at an airport official told Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actor Gene Hackman's death not from carbon monoxide, authorities say
item.description: Authorities investigating the deaths of legendary actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa say they have ruled out carbon monoxide poisoning as the cause.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Bryan Kohberger transcript, Princeton preppy murder, Gabby Petito's last days
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: I-40 to reopen at Tennessee-North Carolina state line
item.description: The four-mile stretch of I-40 on the Tennessee and North Carolina border will reopen to motor traffic Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Hillmer, NewsNation

item.title: Federal agencies plan second email asking workers for weekly accomplishments
item.description: Some agencies plan to send a second Saturday email to federal workers asking them for bullet points on what they did this week, a sign at least some departments plan to continue with the controversial practice ignited by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: To her, Hurricane Helene debris isn’t trash. It is full of memories
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — The tops of dried, bent cornstalks crunch underfoot. Jill Holtz’s gaze is fixed on the ground ahead. She wanders into the nearby woods and weaves between twisted branches. Then, Holtz spots something and starts to riffle through the withered twigs.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US lobster catch drops as crustaceans migrate to colder Canadian waters
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — The U.S. lobster industry's catch keeps sliding as fishermen contend with the northward migration of the valuable crustaceans. The industry is based mostly in Maine, where lobsters are both a cultural signifier and the backbone of the coastal economy.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to sign order to make English official language of US
item.description: President Trump is expected to sign an executive order to make English the official language of the United States, rescinding policies that require federal agencies to provide language assistance for non-English speakers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Attempted murder fugitive busted as 40-year scheme posing as dead college mate unravels
item.description: A fugitive on the run for the attempted murder his estranged wife more than 40 years ago has been captured after his con of assuming a dead man’s identity for decades finally unraveled, the Justice Department announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Volunteers use bullhorns and sirens to warn immigrants when ICE is in their area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just before dawn, 10 people met at a parking lot shared by a laundromat and coffee shop in South Central Los Angeles on what has become a daily mission: Look for immigration officers and warn people of their presence to try to prevent arrests.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump hosts Zelenskyy at the White House and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico kids forced to live in 'underground caves,' court docs say
item.description: A New Mexico home where eight children lived with their two parents was described by state police as looking like "a city trash landfill.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, KRON4

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: After more than four decades, Stephen Craig Campbell, one of the United States' most wanted men, is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, ABC 4

item.title: Economic blackout protest underway Friday
item.description: (AP) — A grassroots organization is encouraging U.S. residents not to spend any money Friday as an act of “economic resistance” to protest what the group's founder sees as the malign influence of billionaires, big corporations and both major political parties on the lives of working Americans.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A look at Louisiana's Mardi Gras festivities through the years
item.description: After a weekslong pre-Lenten bash — complete with elaborate parades that meander through New Orleans, shimmery beads tossed from floats, streets lined with costumed revelers, lavish balls and seafood boils — Louisiana's 2025 Carnival Season is approaching its grand conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FDA cancels meeting to choose flu vaccine strain
item.description: The cancellation of the FDA meeting, alongside a similar postponement last week of a meeting of Centers for Disease Control and Prevention vaccine advisors, is adding to the growing concern among scientists and public health advocates that Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Bloody crime scene photos play key role in trial of man accused in Palestinian American boy's death
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — Photos of a child's bloody body, a black knife holder and other crime scene evidence took center stage Wednesday at the murder and hate crime trial for an Illinois landlord accused of fatally stabbing a 6-year-old Palestinian American boy in 2023.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5
